memoryArtifacts.headline: Clifford, Phillipa De  story
memoryArtifacts.description: When Philippa De Clifford and her twin sister Maud Mathilde were born in 1372, their father, Roger, was 39 and their mother, Maud, was 37. She married William Ferrers and they had 31 children together …

popularStory.headline: Clifford, Phillipa De  story
popularStory.description: When Philippa De Clifford and her twin sister Maud Mathilde were born in 1372, their father, Roger, was 39 and their mother, Maud, was 37. She married William Ferrers and they had 31 children together …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Philippa Clifford於1371年出生在英國維斯特莫藍德布盧加姆 卡素時，她的父親Roger Clifford 2nd Baron Clifford38歲，母親Maud de Beauchamp36歲。她於1388年10月10日與William Ferrers 5th Barron Ferrers of Groby在英國維斯特莫藍德布盧加姆 卡素結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於從1405年7月4日到1416年在英國英格蘭恩布利亞阿波萊比-易-維斯特莫藍德，埋葬在英國英格蘭恩布利亞阿波萊比-易-維斯特莫藍德。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Philippa Clifford于1371年出生在英国維斯特莫藍德布盧加姆 卡素时，她的父亲Roger Clifford 2nd Baron Clifford38岁，母亲Maud de Beauchamp36岁。她于1388年10月10日与William Ferrers 5th Barron Ferrers of Groby在英国維斯特莫藍德布盧加姆 卡素结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于从1405年7月4日到1416年在英国英格兰恩布利亞阿波萊比-易-維斯特莫藍德，埋葬在英国英格兰恩布利亞阿波萊比-易-維斯特莫藍德。

nameMeanings.name: Clifford
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish: habitational name from any of various places called Clifford in Devon, Gloucestershire, and Yorkshire, and in particular Herefordshire. The placename is derived from Old English clif ‘slope’ + ford ‘ford’.

Irish: adoption of the name in 1 above as Anglicization of several Irish names, especially Ó Clúmháin ‘descendant of Clúmhán’, which was the surname of an ecclesiastical family in Sligo and can be traced back to the 12th century. The personal name meant ‘little hairy one’, a diminutive of Irish clúmach ‘hairy’, from clúmh ‘feathers, plumage, down; hair or fur’. Clifford was also adopted for Coleman and in Fermanagh for Crifferty, Clifferty, and Cliffordy, which are Anglicized forms of Mac Raibheartaigh (compare Rafferty ).

History: A powerful Anglo-Norman family of this name in England and Ireland trace their descent from Walter de Clifford, who took the name from Clifford (Castle) in Herefordshire in the 12th century, after acquiring the Clifford barony by marriage.

variants.name: Coleman
meanings: Irish and English: from the Middle English personal name Col(e)man, Old Irish Colmán, earlier Columbán, adopted as Old Norse Kalman. It was introduced into Cumbria, Westmorland, and Yorkshire by Norwegians from Ireland and probably spread widely across England. Ó Colmáin (‘descendant of Colmán’) was the name of an Irish missionary to Europe, also known as Saint Columban(us) (c. 540–615), who founded the monastery of Bobbio in northern Italy in 614. Columbanus is formally a derivative of the Latin for ‘dove’, seen in the name of the 6th-century missionary known in English as Saint Columba (521–597), who converted the Picts to Christianity. This surname is also very common among African Americans.
Irish: from Mac Colmáin or Ó Colmáin ‘son (or descendant) of Colmán’.
Americanized form of Jewish (Ashkenazic)   Kalman   or   Kolman  .

nameMeanings.name: Philippa
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: feminine form of Philip . In England during the Middle Ages the vernacular name Philip was borne by women as well as men, but female bearers were distinguished in Latin records by this form. It was not, however, used as a regular given name until the 19th century.

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Philippa Clifford was born in 1371, in Brougham Castle, Westmorland, England, her father, Roger Clifford 2nd Baron Clifford, was 38 and her mother, Maud de Beauchamp, was 36. She married William Ferrers 5th Barron Ferrers of Groby on 10 October 1388, in Brougham Castle, Westmorland, England. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 2 daughters. She died from 4 July 1405 to 1416, in Appleby-in-Westmorland, Cumbria, England, United Kingdom, and was buried in Appleby-in-Westmorland, Cumbria, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Philippa Clifford est née en 1371, à Brougham Castle, Westmorland, Angleterre, son père, Roger Clifford 2nd Baron Clifford, avait 38 ans et sa mère, Maud de Beauchamp, avait 36 ans. Elle a épousé William Ferrers 5th Barron Ferrers of Groby le 10 octobre 1388, à Brougham Castle, Westmorland, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée du 4 juillet 1405 au 1416, à Appleby-in-Westmorland, Cumbria, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, et a été enterrée à Appleby-in-Westmorland, Cumbria, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Philippa Clifford 1371 in Brougham Castle, Westmorland, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Roger Clifford 2nd Baron Clifford 38 und ihre Mutter Maud de Beauchamp 36. Sie hat am 10. Oktober 1388 in Brougham Castle, Westmorland, England William Ferrers 5th Barron Ferrers of Groby geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb von 4. Juli 1405 bis 1416 in Appleby-in-Westmorland, Cumbria, England, United Kingdom und wurde in Appleby-in-Westmorland, Cumbria, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Philippa Clifford è nata il nel 1371 nel Brougham Castle, Westmorland, England, sua padre, Roger Clifford 2nd Baron Clifford, aveva 38 anni e sua madre, Maud de Beauchamp, aveva 36 anni. Lei sposato William Ferrers 5th Barron Ferrers of Groby il 10 ottobre 1388, nel Brougham Castle, Westmorland, England. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 2 figlie. Morì da 4 luglio 1405 a 1416, nel Appleby-in-Westmorland, Cumbria, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, e fu sepolta a Appleby-in-Westmorland, Cumbria, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Philippa Cliffordが1371年にイギリスウエストモーランドブロウアム カストルで生まれた時，父Roger Clifford 2nd Baron Cliffordは38歳，母Maud de Beauchampは36歳であった。1388年10月10日にイギリスウエストモーランドブロウアム カストルでWilliam Ferrers 5th Barron Ferrers of Grobyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女はから1405年7月4日まで1416年にイギリスイギリスクウンブリアアップレブイ-イン-ウエストモーランドで死去し，イギリスイギリスクウンブリアアップレブイ-イン-ウエストモーランドにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Philippa Clifford(이)가 1371년 영국 웨스트모를랜드 브루그함 카스틀에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Roger Clifford 2nd Baron Clifford(은)는 38세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Maud de Beauchamp(은)는 36세였다. 그녀는 1388년 10월 10일 영국 웨스트모를랜드 브루그함 카스틀에서 William Ferrers 5th Barron Ferrers of Groby(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1405년 7월 4일부터 1416년 까지 영국 잉글랜드 쿠브리아 애플레비-이-베스트모를랜드에서 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 쿠브리아 애플레비-이-베스트모를랜드에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Philippa Clifford nasceu em 1371, em Brougham Castle, Westmorland, Inglaterra, seu pai, Roger Clifford 2nd Baron Clifford, tinha 38 anos e sua mãe, Maud de Beauchamp, tinha 36 anos. Ela casou-se com William Ferrers 5th Barron Ferrers of Groby em 10 de outubro de 1388, em Brougham Castle, Westmorland, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu de 4 de julho de 1405 a 1416, em Appleby-in-Westmorland, Cumbria, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, e foi sepultada em Appleby-in-Westmorland, Cumbria, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Philippa Clifford родилась в 1371 г. в местности броугам кастл, вестморланд, Англия, её отцу по имени Roger Clifford 2nd Baron Clifford было 38 лет, а матери, которую звали Maud de Beauchamp, 36. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени William Ferrers 5th Barron Ferrers of Groby 10 октября 1388 г. в местности броугам кастл, вестморланд, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она умерла с 4 июля 1405 по 1416 в местности эпплеби-ин-вестморланд, кумбря, Англия, Великобритания и была похоронена в местности эпплеби-ин-вестморланд, кумбря, Англия, Великобритания.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Philippa Clifford nació en 1371, en Brougham Castle, Westmorland, Inglaterra, su papá, Roger Clifford 2nd Baron Clifford, tenía 38 años y su mamá, Maud de Beauchamp, tenía 36 años. Se casó con William Ferrers 5th Barron Ferrers of Groby el 10 de octubre de 1388, en Brougham Castle, Westmorland, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió desde 4 de julio de 1405 hasta 1416, en Appleby-in-Westmorland, Cumbria, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, y fue sepultada en Appleby-in-Westmorland, Cumbria, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
